lektorat.unsafeHtml: Corrected HTML looks incomplete – save aborted.
lektorat.batchConfirm:
Check all {n} pages and store results in history?

For large books this can take several minutes.
lektorat.staleResultDropped: Page was edited during analysis — result discarded so your changes are not overwritten. Please re-run the check.
lektorat.blockedUnsavedEdits: Unsaved changes pending — please save first before starting the proofread.
edit.cancelConfirm: Discard unsaved edits? The local draft will be deleted.
edit.shorterConfirm: The new text is significantly shorter ({newLen} instead of {oldLen} characters). Save anyway?
edit.offlineSaved: Offline – draft saved locally. Will upload automatically once connected.
edit.saveFailed: Save failed – draft saved locally. Error: {msg}
edit.saveFailedRetry: Save failed – draft saved locally. Will retry on next connection.
edit.conflict.message: This page was modified on {time} by {user}. Saving anyway will overwrite their changes.
edit.conflict.kept: Conflict: save cancelled, draft kept locally.
edit.conflict.unsavedHint: Conflict: page was modified by {user}. Please save manually to resolve.
edit.conflict.banner: Conflict: {user} modified this page on {time}. Auto-save paused.
edit.status.savingTip: Save in progress – waiting for server acknowledgement.
edit.status.offlineTip: Server unreachable. Draft is held locally; will retry on next connect.
edit.draftRecovery.banner: Unsaved draft from {when} found. Resume or discard?
edit.draftRecovery.discardConfirm: Delete the local draft for good? This cannot be undone.
find.title: Find & Replace
app.switchPageConfirm: Discard unsaved edits and switch page?
app.cacheClearConfirm:
Clear delta cache for this book?

The next full analysis will re-extract all chapters – even unchanged ones. This increases AI cost and runtime.
komplett.confirm:
Start full analysis?

Characters, sociogram, locations, scenes, events and timeline will be re-computed. For large books this can take several minutes.
userSettings.resetConfirm:
Delete all your editing results, book reviews and chats for "{name}"?

Other users are not affected. This cannot be undone.
bookstats.loadError: Could not load statistics. Please try again.
rueckblick.title: Retrospective
rueckblick.upToDate: Up to date – this period's entries haven't changed since the last generation.
rueckblick.empty: No dated entries in this period.
rueckblick.historyEmpty: No retrospectives saved yet. Pick a period above and create your first one.
chat.pageNotLoaded: Page content not loaded – please re-select the page.
chat.originalNotFound: Original text no longer present on the page.
common.noDataYet: No data yet. Please click "Analyze book".
session.expired: Session expired – please sign in again. Unsaved changes in the editor will be lost on reload.
session.serverOffline: Server unreachable – offline mode. Cached content is readable; changes will only be saved once reconnected.
session.offlineMode: Offline – changes are stored locally and will sync once reconnected. AI features paused.
session.updateAvailable: New version available – reload now to use it. Unsaved changes in the editor will be lost.
offline.syncConflicts: {n} draft(s) not synced – server changed in the meantime. You'll be asked when you reopen the page.
dev.banner: DEV MODE – local session, no OAuth
app.subtitle: Writing, editing and book analysis with AI
book.label: Book
welcome.title: Welcome to Schreibwerkstatt
welcome.hint: Create your first book to get started. Then you can add chapters and pages, write, and have your text analysed.
book.create.title: Create new book
book.settings.title: Settings
publication.authorNameHint: Shown as author in PDF/EPUB. Empty = account name.
publication.authorFileAsHint: For catalogue/reader sorting (Last, First). Empty = same as author name.
publication.coAuthorsHint: Writing duos: each person gets its own author entry in the EPUB metadata.
publication.subtitle: Subtitle
publication.tradeHint: Written into the EPUB metadata (OPF) — used by reader apps, online stores and library catalogues.
publication.keywordsHint: Comma-separated — one dc:subject entry per term.
publication.matterHint: Free front/back-matter pages for the EPUB: newsletter call-out, also-by, review request, sample chapter, acknowledgements, content warnings.
book.settings.name: Book title
book.settings.contextPlaceholder: e.g. Setting: Zurich, 1970s. Atmosphere: dark, melancholic. Notes: the protagonist suffers from amnesia – memory gaps are intentional.
book.settings.pov.kurzgeschichtenHint: Note: For short-story collections, perspective and tense act only as guidelines. Individual stories may legitimately deviate – the AI will not flag those as errors.
book.settings.finished.desc: Hides the daily writing tiles in the book overview (trend, 7-day bars, today ring, streak heatmap).
book.settings.dailyGoalCharsHint: Target value for the Today ring in the header and on the book overview. 1500 characters ≈ 1 standard page.
book.settings.orteReal.desc: Unlocks a geographic map in the Locations card (coordinates via OpenStreetMap). Leave off for fictional worlds.
book.settings.schauplatzLand.hint: Country where the book mainly takes place. Improves location placement (geocoding bias) and gives the AI a country default during analysis.
stt.error.permission: Microphone access denied. Please allow it in your browser.
stt.error.unavailable: No microphone available or speech recognition unsupported.
entities.toggle.hint: Mark characters/locations in text, show scenes/events per page
entities.panel.title: Context
entities.empty.runAnalysis: No entities extracted yet. Run the full analysis so characters, locations, scenes and events show up here.
book.settings.dangerText: Deletes all your page edits, book reviews and chats for this book. Other users are not affected. This cannot be undone.
book.settings.deleteBookText: Permanently deletes the book and all app references (content, page edits, reviews, chats, characters, locations, scenes, continuity, timeline, statistics, writing and editing time, PDF profiles — for all users of this book).
book.settings.deleteBookConfirm:
Delete the book “{name}”?

ALL data for this book is removed permanently — including other users'.
book.share.title: Access & Sharing
book.share.lektorBookChatDesc: Default: book chat is editor+ only. Enable to let proof-readers use it (higher token cost).
book.share.inviteSent: Invitation sent to {email}. Once they have registered, you can share the book.
book.share.transferConfirm: Transfer ownership to {email}? You will become editor and lose sharing rights.
page.lock.banner: {email} is proof-reading this page (until {expires}). Editing paused.
page.lock.lektorHint: You are proof-reading — others cannot edit this page right now.
book.export.title: Export
book.export.hint: Downloads book, chapter, or page as a file. Filename is timestamped.
publication.exportToolsHint: Layout, fonts, cover and reflow settings are managed in the customizable export cards.
book.export.migrationHint: Export the whole book as a .swbook file and read it back in on another instance via «Import». Always includes pages, chapter structure and book settings. Optionally also:
book.export.migrate.analysis.desc: Figures, locations, scenes, songs, world facts, timeline, continuity and ideas.
book.export.migrate.lektorat.desc: Saved page checks (findings, style analysis, verdict).
book.export.running: Export running ({fmt}) – may take up to a minute for large books …
export.scope.label: Scope
pdfExport.title: PDF export (custom)
pdfExport.empty: No profile yet. Create one or clone an existing one.
pdfExport.includeSubchaptersTip: Include all subchapters when exporting a chapter (tree order, depth-first).
pdfExport.pdfaWarning: PDF generated, but PDF/A validator reported issues. File was delivered anyway.
pdfExport.pdfxWarning: PDF generated, but PDF/X conversion was unavailable (Ghostscript/ICC missing). Unconverted PDF was delivered.
pdfExport.coverInInteriorWarning: PDF generated. Note: the print-ready interior contains an inner cover — for printing, use the separate cover PDF instead.
pdfExport.tab.author: Author
pdfExport.layout.bodyInsetHint: Extra inset for body text only (paragraphs, lists, quotes). Headings, images and rules keep the page margin.
pdfExport.layout.tokensHint: Tokens: {title} = book title · {author} = author · {chapter} = chapter name · {pageTitle} = page name · {page} = current page number · {pages} = total pages
pdfExport.layout.mirrorMarginsHint: When active: left margin = gutter (inner) on right-hand pages, swapped on left-hand pages. Header/footer mirror accordingly.
pdfExport.layout.hyphenateHint: Breaks long words in justified text using DE/EN hyphenation patterns. Reduces rivers and large word gaps.
pdfExport.layout.widowOrphanHint: Prevents single trailing or leading lines stranded at the top/bottom of the next page. Measures paragraph height before printing and pushes the whole paragraph to the next page when necessary.
pdfExport.layout.versoSlotsHint: Slots used only on even (left-hand) pages. Empty = the recto slot is used on verso as well.
pdfExport.font.heading: Headings
pdfExport.font.title: Book title
pdfExport.font.subtitle: Subtitle
pdfExport.font.colorHintRule: also the color of the chapter/page rule
pdfExport.font.previewText: Once upon a time there was a king with three sons …
pdfExport.chapter.unnumberedHint: These chapters appear without a number in the heading and in the table of contents. Numbering continues without gaps. Sub-chapters of a marked chapter inherit the no-number setting.
pdfExport.chapter.skipPageCounterChapter: Pages in these chapters do not count toward the footer page counter (display + count suppressed, next regular page continues without a jump). Sub-chapters inherit the setting.
pdfExport.chapter.skipPageCounterPagesHi: Exclude individual BookStack pages from the counter. Only effective when “page break between pages” is active (otherwise the pages cannot be told apart in the layout).
pdfExport.chapter.pageStructureHint: Decide whether the individual pages within a chapter appear as sub-headings or render continuously.
pdfExport.cover.spineHint: Produces a print-ready cover sheet: back + spine + front. Front = the title image from Book settings → Publication. Bleed and crop marks are taken from the Print tab.
pdfExport.cover.imageFromPublication: The title image is maintained book-wide under Book settings → Publication (shared with the EPUB export).
pdfExport.cover.paperBulkHint: Spine thickness per 1000 interior pages in mm — from the printer / paper datasheet. Typically 60–90.
pdfExport.cover.spineNeedsValues: Enter page count and paper bulk to compute the spine width.
pdfExport.toc.title: Heading
pdfExport.toc.format.heading: Formatting
pdfExport.extras.hint: Render placement. Title-page content (ISBN, subtitle, year, dedication, imprint, copyright, frontmatter, bio) + cover/author photo live under Book settings → Publication.
pdfExport.extras.subtitle: Subtitle (optional)
pdfExport.extras.barcodeHint: Print-ready barcode on the imprint page. Only with a valid 13-digit ISBN.
pdfExport.extras.frontMatterHint: Appears as its own page after the title page (e.g. epigraph, motto, short foreword).
pdfExport.author.hint: Optional «About the author» page at the end of the book. Photo and text are both optional.
pdfExport.print.hint: Prepress for a classic print shop. Bleed + crop marks only needed if the printer requires them.
pdfExport.print.trimPresetHint: Sets the trim size (overrides page size with «custom»).
pdfExport.print.blackTextKOnlyHint: Takes effect only with CMYK/PDF-X (phase 3). No effect today.
pdfExport.print.lowResWarning: {count} image(s) below the dpi threshold — may be too low-res for print.
pdfExport.norm.hintPdfa: Machine-readable archival format, sRGB. Validation runs server-side (veraPDF) when available.
pdfExport.norm.hintPdfx: Prepress standard for the print shop. RGB is preserved and an output-intent profile (PSO Uncoated v3 / FOGRA52) is embedded — the shop separates to CMYK itself. Conversion runs server-side via Ghostscript when available.
pdfExport.norm.hintNone: Plain PDF without any standard marking.
pdfExport.error.coverInvalid: Cover image is invalid or too large.
pdfExport.error.authorImageInvalid: Author photo is invalid or too large.
pdfExport.error.backCoverInvalid: Back cover image is invalid or too large.
pdfExport.error.PROFILE_NAME_TAKEN: A profile with this name already exists.
job.error.coverSpecRequired: Enter page count and paper bulk to compute the spine width.
palette.hero.text: Search, jump or run a command …
tile.overview.title: Show book overview
tile.figuren.title: Show character overview
tile.plot.desc: Plan the plot as a beat board
plot.title: Plot Workshop
plot.empty.hint: Sketch your plot as a beat board: acts as columns, plot points as cards. Start by adding an act.
plot.brainstorm.title: Suggestions
plot.consistency.title: Consistency check
plot.consistency.noFindings: No conflicts found — the plan is consistent.
plot.tension.title: Tension arc
plot.thread.hint: Multiple main characters? Add story threads — the board becomes an acts × threads grid.
plot.thread.confirmDelete: Delete thread “{name}”? Its {n} beat(s) stay (they fall into “no thread”).
plot.thread.ownActsTip: Give this thread its own act structure (clones the shared acts)
plot.thread.sharedActsTip: Back to the shared acts (dissolve the own act structure)
plot.thread.forkNoActs: Create at least one shared act first.
plot.thread.confirmFork: Give thread “{name}” its own act structure? The shared acts are copied; from then on this thread plans independently.
plot.thread.confirmUnfork: Dissolve the own act structure of “{name}”? The beats move back onto the shared acts, the own acts are deleted.
plot.error.load: Could not load the plot board.
plot.error.brainstorm: Could not start the brainstorm job.
plot.error.consistency: Could not start the consistency check.
plot.error.runLoad: Could not load the consistency check.
plot.error.boardEmpty: The board is empty — add beats first.
tile.szenen.title: Show scene analysis
tile.stats.title: Show book progression
tile.bookchat.title: Chat with the whole book
tile.events.title: Show global timeline
tile.weltfakten.title: World facts and rules of the book
tile.orte.title: Locations of the book
tile.songs.title: Music in the book (songs, artists, who listens to what)
tile.kontinuitaet.title: Continuity check: find contradictions in the book
tile.tagebuchRueckblick.title: AI retrospective: themes, people, places and notable days of a period
tile.stil.title: Deterministic style metrics per chapter
tile.fehlerHeatmap.title: Issue types per chapter from the editing runs
tile.bookSettings.title: Configure language and region for this book
tile.finetuneExport.title: Generate JSONL training data from this book for Mistral fine-tuning
tile.export.title: Download the entire book as a file
tile.epubExport.desc: Scope, reflow style, table of contents
epubExport.title: EPUB export (custom)
epubExport.hint: Generates an EPUB with cover, front matter and author bio. Pick the scope and reflow settings.
epubExport.checkWarning: EPUB created, but the validator (EPUBCheck) reports issues. The file was delivered anyway.
epubExport.includeSubchaptersTip: When exporting a chapter, also include all sub-chapters (tree order, depth-first).
epubExport.metaHint: Cover, front matter and author bio are maintained book-wide:
epubExport.error.network: Network error while starting the EPUB export.
epubExport.metadataHint: Written to the EPUB metadata (OPF) — read by reader apps and catalogues.
epubExport.field.chapterPagebreak: Start chapters on a new page
epubExport.field.nestPagesInToc: Show pages in table of contents
epubExport.field.unnumberedHint: These chapters appear without a number in the heading and in the table of contents. Numbering continues without gaps. Sub-chapters of a marked chapter inherit the no-number setting.
epubExport.field.chapterNumberDivider: Rule between chapter number and title
epubExport.field.subchapterPagebreak: Start sub-chapters on a new page
epubExport.imprintPosition.back: Back (at the end of the book)
tile.bookOrganizer.title: Reorder, create, rename and delete chapters and pages
tile.bookEditor.desc: Edit the whole book as one stream
tile.bookEditor.title: Edit all chapters and pages in a single scrollable view with book-wide find/replace
tile.search.title: Full-text search across pages, chapters, characters, locations, scenes and ideas
tile.folderImport.desc: Import a diary folder (ZIP) or an export bundle (.swbook)
tile.folderImport.title: Upload a ZIP archive with daily files (.docx, .odt) or a .swbook bundle
folderImport.title: Import
folderImport.subtitle: Diary ZIP or export bundle (.swbook)
folderImport.swbookHint: Read in a .swbook bundle exported via «Move book». A new book is created; you become its owner. Full analysis, editing and chats contained in the bundle are restored automatically.
folderImport.grouping.label: Chapter structure
folderImport.grouping.hint.year-month: One chapter per year, one sub-chapter per month, one page per day.
folderImport.grouping.hint.year: One chapter per year, all days directly underneath.
folderImport.grouping.hint.flat: No chapters — all pages directly in the book, sorted chronologically.
folderImport.dropZone: Drop ZIP file here or click to select
folderImport.help.chapters: Chapters are created per year, pages per day (date as page name)
folderImport.result.title: Import finished
job.error.importBufferMissing: Import buffer no longer available (TTL expired).
job.error.emptyArchive: ZIP archive empty or contains no valid files.
job.error.unsupportedVersion: This .swbook file is from a newer version and cannot be imported.
search.title: Full-text search
search.subtitle: Pages, chapters, characters, locations, scenes, ideas
bookEditor.title: Book editor
bookEditor.btn.findTitle: Find & replace across the whole book (Cmd/Ctrl+F)
bookEditor.outline.title: Outline
bookEditor.conflict.banner: This page was modified by {user} in the meantime.
bookEditor.find.title: Find & replace in book
bookOrganizer.title: Book organizer
bookOrganizer.hint: Drag to reorder. Click a name to rename. Tab/Shift+Tab in the chapter field indents/outdents (max 3 levels).
bookOrganizer.chapterHasSubchapters: Chapter "{name}" still has sub-chapters. Please resolve or move them first.
bookOrganizer.empty: Book has no pages or chapters yet.
bookOrganizer.noPagesInChapter: Chapter empty. Drop here or add a page.
bookOrganizer.chapterNotEmpty: Chapter "{name}" still contains {n} pages. Please move or delete all pages first.
bookOrganizer.pageInEditorWarn: Page is open in the editor. Please close it first.
overview.title: Overview
overview.empty: No data yet. Load pages or start a full analysis.
overview.streak.title: Writing stats (52 weeks)
overview.today.title: Today
overview.rueckblickHeatmap.title: Retrospective coverage
sidebar.checkAllTitle: Check all pages sequentially and store results in history
diary.anniversary.title: On this day
diary.anniversary.empty: Nothing written on this day in earlier years.
diary.range.title: Search a date range
sidebar.emptyChapterTitle: Open chapter "{name}" to add the first page
sidebar.emptyBook: This book has no chapters or pages yet.
sidebar.resizeHandle: Resize sidebar (use arrow keys to adjust)
komplett.title: Full analysis
jobStats.title: Job statistics
jobQueue.label: Jobs:
review.title: Book review
kapitelReview.title: Chapter review
kapitelReview.includeSubchaptersTip: When enabled, all pages of sub-chapters are part of the review.
kapitelReview.emptyHint: This chapter has no pages yet — add the first one now.
figuren.title: Characters
figuren.heading: Characters
figuren.graph.hint: Horizontal = chapter sequence · Vertical = character type · Bar under the node = per-chapter presence (darker = more frequent) · Click a chapter title to filter
figuren.soziogramm.hint: Arrow thickness = power asymmetry · Node rows = social class · Arrow direction = stronger → weaker · Within a class: powerful at top, subordinates at bottom
werkstatt.title: Character Workshop
werkstatt.subline: Forward draft — not linked to extracted characters
werkstatt.empty.title: No workshop characters yet
werkstatt.empty.hint: Create a first character and develop it as a mindmap.
werkstatt.field.name: Name
werkstatt.archetype.placeholder: Choose an archetype or leave blank
werkstatt.error.applyFailed: Could not attach suggestion — node was removed in the meantime.
werkstatt.action.brainstormSelectFirst: Select a node in the mindmap first
werkstatt.mindmap.title: Mindmap
werkstatt.import.placeholder: Pick a character from the book
werkstatt.import.empty: No importable characters — every book character already has a workshop draft.
werkstatt.importedFrom.tip: This workshop draft was imported from a book character. AI brainstorming and consistency checks exclude the source character from the book context so it cannot contradict itself.
werkstatt.error.importLoad: Importable characters could not be loaded.
werkstatt.error.knotenGone: Node no longer exists in this mindmap. Suggestion cannot be applied.
werkstatt.error.runsLoad: AI run history could not be loaded.
tile.werkstatt.title: Build characters as a mindmap
graph.empty.sozialschicht:
No social classes analysed yet.
Please click "Analyze book" to determine class membership and power structures.
szenen.title: Scenes
events.title: Events
events.estimatedHint: Year inferred from context (not explicitly stated in the text)
events.timelineUndated: {count} events without a year are listed below only
events.timelineUndatedHint: Jump to the first undated event
orte.title: Locations
weltfakten.title: World facts
weltfakten.empty: No world facts yet. Run the full analysis.
orte.map.noResult: No match for “{name}” — set the marker manually.
orte.map.pinDropped: No match for “{name}” — pin dropped at map center. Drag it to the right spot.
orte.map.dragHint: Drag a marker to correct its position.
orte.map.undoTip: Undo pin move — up to 10 steps
orte.map.lockTip: Lock located markers against accidental dragging
orte.map.lockedHint: Located markers are locked — open the lock above to adjust them. Unlocated pins stay draggable.
orte.map.unresolved: No match for “{name}” — drag the pin in the map centre to the right spot.
orte.map.batchDone: {hits} of {total} located. Pins without a match sit in the map centre — drag them to the right spot.
orte.map.clearAllConfirm: Remove the geo-references of all {n} located places? The places themselves stay; only the coordinates are cleared.
orte.col.name: Name
songs.title: Music
kontinuitaet.title: Continuity
kontinuitaet.noMatch: No issues matching the selected filter.
bookstats.title: Book progress
bookstats.emptyState: No data yet. Click "Synchronise" to capture the first snapshot.
stil.title: Style heatmap
stil.emptyState: No style metrics yet. Click "Recompute" to derive them from the pages.
stil.legend: Red = high value (usually worse), green = low. Readability (LIX/Flesch) has its own direction: higher Flesch = easier to read.
stil.tip.filler_per1k: Filler words per 1000 words (curated list, German).
stil.tip.sentence_len_p90: 90th percentile of sentence lengths — indicator for long sentences.
stil.tip.dialog_ratio: Share of direct speech by character count.
stil.tip.repetition_score: Sum of top-10 word repetitions per 1000 words (excluding stopwords).
stil.tip.lix: LIX readability index (Björnsson): average sentence length + share of long words (>6 letters). Higher = harder. <40 easy, 40–50 medium, 50–60 hard, >60 very hard (official/technical text).
stil.tip.flesch_de: Amstad-Flesch readability index for German: based on average sentence length and syllables per word. Higher = easier to read. 90–100 very easy, 60–70 standard/newspaper, 30–50 hard, <30 very hard.
stil.detail.title: {metric} — {chapter}
stil.detail.empty: No concrete examples stored for this chapter.
fehlerHeatmap.title: Issue heatmap
fehlerHeatmap.emptyState: No editing runs yet. Open a page and run a check.
fehlerHeatmap.legend: Cell number = findings of this issue type in the chapter. Colour per column: red = many compared to the rest of the book, green = few. Hatched = chapter not yet or only partially checked. Tooltip shows page count and findings per 1000 words.
fehlerHeatmap.detail.title: {typ} — {chapter}
editor.title: {name}
editor.draftTitle: Local draft – not yet on the server
collab.tree.remoteEditTip: Another user recently saved this page.
collab.presence.bannerOne: {user} is also editing this page right now.
collab.presence.bannerMany: {count} others are also editing this page right now.
collab.bookAccessLost: This book is no longer available (deleted or access revoked).
presence.self.editing: You are editing this page on {device}.
presence.self.bannerOne: You are also editing this page on {device}.
presence.self.bannerMany: You are editing this page on {count} other devices.
editor.revisions.restoreConfirm: Set the version from {when} as current? A new revision will be created.
editor.revisions.restorePrevTitle: Restore the state that existed before this edit
editor.revisions.viewer.title: Revision #{n}
editor.revisions.viewer.noChanges: No differences from the previous version.
editor.focus.exitConflict: Focus could not be saved cleanly
editor.notebook.label: Writing notebook
chat.title: Page chat
chat.emptyPage: Start the conversation with a question about the page "{name}".
chat.emptyBook: Ask a question about the whole book "{name}". The assistant searches the book index and loads relevant passages on demand.
chat.inputHint: Enter to send  ·  Shift+Enter for line break
chat.errors.contextExceeded: The response was cut short due to context limits. Please narrow your question.
chat.errors.maxIterReached: I ran out of tool calls before finding an answer. Please phrase the question more concretely.
header.updateAllTitle: Re-run figures, sociogram, locations, scene analysis, events and timeline
header.clearCacheTitle: Clear delta cache for this book – next analysis re-extracts all chapters
shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.item.escape: Close open dialogs, menus and pickers
shortcuts.item.bossScreen: Boss key: instantly black out the screen (in the editor); any key/click brings it back
shortcuts.item.figurLookup: Character lookup by clicking on a name
shortcuts.item.find: Find & Replace (in edit mode); otherwise focus book search
shortcuts.item.focusRecenter: Move cursor line to viewport center (focus mode)
shortcuts.item.treeArrows: In the page tree, walk with ↑/↓, Enter selects
shortcuts.item.findingsNav: In review: jump to next/previous finding
shortcuts.item.mindmapNav: Select node / move to siblings & parent
shortcuts.item.mindmapAddChild: Add a child to the selected node
shortcuts.item.mindmapMenu: Open context menu (rename, add nodes, brainstorm, delete)
shortcuts.item.mindmapBlur: Leave mindmap focus (Tab stays inside otherwise)
shortcuts.item.slashMenu: Slash menu in empty block (notebook editor)
shortcuts.item.findNext: In find input: next / previous match
shortcuts.item.treeHomeEnd: In page tree: first / last page
shortcuts.footer: Press ? to open, Esc to close.
admin.settings.title: App settings
admin.settings.api.intro: Bearer tokens for external scrapers (Prometheus, Home Assistant, Grafana). The endpoint serves all admin-console metrics in Prometheus text format.
admin.settings.api.justCreatedHint: This value is shown only once. The database stores only a hash — copy it now or you will need to create a new token.
admin.settings.api.namePlaceholder: e.g. Prometheus prod / HA living room
admin.settings.api.expiresHint: Empty = never expires. Recommended for temporary setups (testing, demo).
admin.settings.api.col.name: Name
admin.settings.api.confirmRevoke: Revoke this token? Scrapers using it will fail immediately.
admin.settings.api.snippetsHint: Copy the token above and paste it into the snippet.
admin.settings.languagetool.hint: Self-hosted LanguageTool server (Docker: erikvl87/languagetool). Activates the spellcheck overlay in all three editors and disables the browser's native spellcheck.
admin.settings.languagetool.url.label: LT server URL
admin.settings.languagetool.url.help: Full URL without path, e.g. http://lt.lan:8010. Must expose /v2/check and /v2/languages.
admin.settings.languagetool.picky.label: Picky mode (stricter style rules)
admin.settings.languagetool.picky.help: Sends level=picky to LT. More hits, higher false-positive rate.
admin.settings.languagetool.debounce.hel: Wait time between last keystroke and LT check in the three editors. Lower = faster squiggles, more server load. Range 200–10,000 ms, default 1500.
admin.settings.stt.hint: Self-hosted speech-to-text (OpenAI-compatible Whisper endpoint, e.g. Speaches / faster-whisper-server). Shows a dictation microphone button in the notebook editor. Audio is only forwarded, never stored; host/key never leave the server.
admin.settings.stt.enabled.label: Dictation active
admin.settings.stt.host.label: Whisper host URL
admin.settings.stt.host.help: Base URL without path, e.g. http://whisper.local:8000. The /v1/audio/transcriptions endpoint is appended automatically.
admin.settings.stt.model.label: Model
admin.settings.stt.model.help: Model ID of the Whisper server.
admin.settings.stt.apiKey.label: API key (optional)
admin.settings.stt.apiKey.help: Optional bearer token for hosted endpoints. Empty = no Authorization header (local servers).
admin.settings.stt.language.label: Fallback language
admin.settings.stt.language.help: Only used when there is no book scope. Otherwise the book language wins.
admin.settings.stt.temperature.label: Temperature
admin.settings.stt.temperature.help: Sampling temperature of the Whisper model. Range 0–1, default 0 (deterministic, fewer hallucinations).
admin.settings.stt.vadSilenceMs.label: VAD silence (ms)
admin.settings.stt.vadSilenceMs.help: Duration of the speech pause after which a segment is cut and transcribed. Range 200–5000 ms, default 800.
admin.settings.stt.vadThreshold.label: VAD threshold
admin.settings.stt.vadThreshold.help: RMS volume threshold for speech vs. silence. Range 0–1, default 0.015.
admin.settings.stt.vadMaxSegmentS.label: Max. segment length (s)
admin.settings.stt.vadMaxSegmentS.help: Forces a cut during continuous speech without a pause. Range 5–120 s, default 30.
admin.settings.geocode.hint: Source for the locations map's coordinate lookup. Nominatim (OSM) runs public or self-hosted; Photon (Komoot) is self-hosted. No hit = manual pin via marker drag is always possible.
admin.settings.geocode.provider.label: Provider
admin.settings.geocode.nominatim.label: Nominatim URL
admin.settings.geocode.nominatim.help: Full search endpoint. Public instance: max. 1 request/second (serialized server-side). Self-hosted has no limit.
admin.settings.geocode.photon.label: Photon URL
admin.settings.geocode.photon.help: Base URL of the Photon instance without path (e.g. http://photon.lan:2322). The /api endpoint is appended automatically.
admin.settings.geocode.tilesHint: Source of the map imagery. Leaflet fetches the tiles directly in the browser. Default = public OSM (mind the tile usage policy); your own tile server takes load off the public OSM servers and fits the self-hosting philosophy.
admin.settings.geocode.tiles.label: Tile server URL
admin.settings.geocode.tiles.help: Map tile URL in {z}/{x}/{y}.png scheme (Leaflet fetches tiles directly in the browser). Clear the field and it falls back to the public OSM server (https://{s}.tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png) — then mind the OSM tile usage policy. Self-hosted tile server (e.g. openstreetmap-tile-server or tileserver-gl): enter your own URL, many serve under /tile/ — example: http://tiles.lan:8080/tile/{z}/{x}/{y}.png. The {s} subdomain is optional. An http://-only tile server (e.g. on your LAN) is automatically served through the app's own proxy — it needs neither HTTPS nor to be exposed.
Copyright notice shown at the bottom-right of the map. Empty = default OSM attribution. Adjust for a self-hosted tile server.
admin.settings.analytics.scriptUrlHint: Full URL to the Plausible tracker JS, e.g. https://analytics.example.com/js/pa-XXXX.js. The origin is added to the CSP automatically. Tracking runs on every host as soon as the checkbox is enabled.
admin.settings.smtp.hint: Gmail account for invite + notification mails. Active as soon as address and App Password are set. Host/port/TLS are implicit via service:'gmail' (smtp.gmail.com:465 TLS).
admin.settings.smtp.appPasswordHint: 2FA must be enabled. Generate the 16-character app password at:
admin.settings.mail.adminRecipientHint: Forwarding address for admin notifications (job crash, token cap, budget overrun). Empty = sent to all active admin users. Set = replaces that list entirely; the address does not need to match an app account.
admin.settings.mail.notifyHint: Master toggles per notification path. Only effective when SMTP is configured.
admin.settings.mail.skipErrorsHint: Comma-separated list of error/i18n keys that do NOT trigger a crash mail. Empty = defaults from lib/notify.js.
admin.settings.auth.captchaHint: Self-hosted proof-of-work captcha (no third party). Protects /register and the ENV admin login against bots. Active = the browser invisibly solves a compute puzzle on submit; the HMAC secret is generated automatically when enabled. The hard rate limits stay active regardless.
admin.settings.help.altchaComplexity: Difficulty of the compute puzzle (upper search bound). Higher = more bot cost, but a slightly slower solve in the browser. Default 100,000.
admin.settings.help.registrationExpireDa: Maximum age of pending registration requests in days. After that the cron marks them 'expired'.
admin.settings.help.appName: Display name of the app (mail templates, landing page, startup log).
admin.settings.advanced.disclaimer: Leave defaults unless you have a strong reason — values are calibrated from practice.
admin.settings.help.providerChoice: Default provider for users without their own override. Per-user override in user management.
admin.settings.help.appTimezone: IANA time zone (e.g. Europe/Zurich). Applies to cron jobs, server date buckets (streak, page_stats), and all GUI date formatters — browser time zone is overridden.
admin.settings.help.contextWindow: Full model context window (input + output) in tokens. For local models, set this to the native context size (Mistral-Small3.2 / Gemma3 / Llama-3.1: 128 000; older: 32 000 / 8 000).
admin.settings.help.maxTokensOut: Output cap in tokens. Must be well below `context_window` (the rest = input budget). Per-job overrides are capped on top.
admin.settings.komplett.title: Impact on the full analysis
admin.settings.komplett.derived: Derived from this: single-pass up to {single} characters, otherwise split into chunks of {chunk} characters (input budget {budget} characters).
admin.settings.komplett.warn: Recommended for the full analysis: at least {recommended} tokens of context window. With {ctx} tokens a longer book gets split into small chunks — characters, facts and continuity are fragmented across many passes, which lowers extraction quality.
admin.settings.komplett.bad: {ctx} tokens of context window is very tight for the full analysis: even short books are shredded into tiny chunks of {chunk} characters, and relationships and continuity across chapter boundaries are lost. At least {recommended} tokens strongly recommended (the native size of modern local models such as Mistral-Small3.2 / Gemma3 / Llama-3.1).
admin.settings.help.repeatPenalty: Anti-loop: penalises recently repeated tokens and breaks repetition loops (e.g. during JSON extraction). 1.0 = off, 1.1–1.2 mild recommended. Values too high can damage valid JSON.
admin.settings.help.think: Enable reasoning/thinking. Off (recommended): the model does not think, saving output tokens. On: models like Qwen3 or DeepSeek-R1 emit a reasoning trace that we discard — costs tokens and time.
admin.settings.help.thinkOpenaiCompat: Enable reasoning/thinking. Off (recommended): sends `chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking=false` to suppress the reasoning trace on vLLM/SGLang/llama.cpp (Qwen3 & co), saving output tokens. On: nothing is sent (model default) — use this for real OpenAI, which rejects unknown fields.
admin.settings.help.openaiCompatApiKey: Optional bearer token for hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints (vLLM, LiteLLM, OpenAI). Leave empty for local servers without auth (e.g. llama.cpp).
admin.settings.help.komplettExtractMaxTo: Output-token limit for the full-analysis AI calls (extraction, consolidation, continuity). Effective only for local models (Ollama / openai-compat) — caps their generation length to protect VRAM/latency. Ignored when `ai.provider = claude` (Claude caps at the provider ceiling since only generated tokens are billed).
admin.settings.claude.komplettIntro: Applies only to the full-analysis family (extraction, consolidation, continuity). Empty field / 0 = follows the standard values above. Lets the book analysis run e.g. on Opus with more output, while standard calls (editing, chat) stay on the global model.
admin.settings.help.komplettModel: Claude model used only for the full analysis (extraction, consolidation, continuity). Empty = follows `ai.claude.model`. Lets you run e.g. Opus for the more thorough book analysis while Sonnet stays global (e.g. for editing). Only applies when `ai.provider = claude`.
admin.settings.help.komplettContextWindo: Context window for the full analysis only. 0 = follows `ai.claude.context_window`. Higher = larger books run single-pass (more accurate). Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.x support up to 1000000.
admin.settings.help.komplettMaxTokensOut: Output cap for the full analysis only. 0 = follows `ai.claude.max_tokens_out`. Must match the full-analysis model: Sonnet 4.6 ≤ 64000, Opus 4.8 ≤ 128000 — too high returns HTTP 400. Lets the analysis use more output on Opus without the global (e.g. Sonnet) calls exceeding the 64K limit.
admin.settings.help.komplettTimeoutMs: Hard timeout per Claude call for the full analysis only (milliseconds). 0 = follows `ai.claude.timeout_ms`. Opus is slower and the single-pass makes several large calls — so the book analysis often needs more than the global 600000 (10 min). Example: 2400000 = 40 min. Max 3600000 (60 min).
admin.settings.claude.bookchatIntro: Applies only to the book chat (classic + agentic tool loop). Empty field / 0 = follows the standard values above. Lets the agentic book chat run e.g. on Opus (better tool selection + multi-step reasoning), while standard calls (editing, full analysis) stay on the global model.
admin.settings.help.bookchatModel: Claude model used only for the book chat. Empty = follows `ai.claude.model`. Lets you run e.g. Opus for the agentic tool loop while Sonnet stays global (e.g. for editing). Only applies when `ai.provider = claude`.
admin.settings.help.bookchatContextWindo: Context window for the book chat only. 0 = follows `ai.claude.context_window`. Higher = more book text / tool results per answer. Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.x support up to 1000000.
admin.settings.help.bookchatMaxTokensOut: Output cap for the book chat only. 0 = follows `ai.claude.max_tokens_out`. Must match the book-chat model: Sonnet 4.6 ≤ 64000, Opus 4.8 ≤ 128000 — too high returns HTTP 400.
admin.settings.help.bookchatTimeoutMs: Hard timeout per Claude call for the book chat only (milliseconds). 0 = follows `ai.claude.timeout_ms`. The global 10-min default is usually enough (one tool-use round per call). Max 3600000 (60 min).
admin.settings.help.completenessPasses: Claude single-pass only: how many extra passes after the first extraction specifically hunt for missed characters, locations, facts and scenes (long-tail recall). 0 = off. Stops early once a round finds nothing new. Higher = more thorough, but more tokens/time.
admin.settings.help.charsPerToken: Scales char-to-token estimates. Provider-agnostic. Default 3.0 is calibrated for German.
admin.settings.help.pageRevisionLimit: Floor for page-revisions retention: newest N revisions per page are always kept on top of the GFS schema (24h / 1 per day / 1 per week / 1 per month / 1 per year, oldest of each). Cleanup runs daily.
admin.settings.help.advancedClaude: Control retries, timeout and parallelism of the Claude calls against Anthropic TPM limits.

job.error.gesamtnoteMissing: AI response invalid: overall grade missing.
job.warn.soziogrammDegraded: Sociogram refinement skipped – preliminary values used.
job.warn.figurenKonsolidierungDegraded: Character consolidation skipped (response too long) – rule-based merged characters used.
job.warn.orteKonsolidierungDegraded: Location consolidation skipped (response too long) – per-chapter extracted locations used.
job.warn.songsKonsolidierungDegraded: Song consolidation skipped (response too long) – per-chapter extracted songs used.
job.warn.relationsFailed: Relationship analysis failed – characters saved without relationships.
job.warn.faktenFailed: Fact extraction failed – the rest of the catalog was updated.
job.warn.eventsFailed: Life-event extraction failed – the rest of the catalog was updated.

job.warn.continuityFailed: Continuity check skipped – the rest of the catalog was updated.
job.warn.zeitstrahlDegraded: Timeline consolidation skipped – events saved as raw groups.
job.warn.timelineFailed: Timeline phase failed – the rest of the catalog was updated.
job.warn.factsChapterSkipped: Facts for some chapters could not be extracted ({chapters}) – the continuity check ran on a reduced fact base.
job.warn.chunksTruncated: {count} text section(s) could not be fully analysed by the model (repetition loop) and were skipped: {chapters}. The remaining sections were processed normally.
job.phase.extracting: AI extracting characters, locations, facts, scenes…
job.phase.verifyContradictions: AI verifying contradictions against the source text…
job.error.rueckblickEmpty: No retrospective possible: no usable entries in this period.
job.error.werkstatt.vorschlaegeMissing: AI response did not contain suggestions.
job.error.werkstatt.konflikteMissing: AI response did not contain conflicts array.

finetune.title: Fine-tuning export
finetune.lead: Generates JSONL training data from book text, characters, events, scenes, and locations. Chat format (messages) plus optional pre-rendered Tekken-V7 text field, loads directly in Unsloth Studio or the Unsloth CLI. Defaults tuned for Mistral-Small-3.2-24B (131072-token context, full-chapter samples enabled). For QLoRA on 20-24 GB set max_seq_tokens to 4096.
finetune.type.style.desc: Paragraph start → continuation. Many samples, teaches the author's tone.
finetune.type.scene.desc: Metadata (characters · location · chapter) → scene text, plus full-chapter completions, sliding-window cuts (start/middle/end), multi-page and chapter-to-chapter continuations, and cloze fill-ins. Uses full-analysis data.
finetune.type.verbatim.desc: Page/chapter → exact full text, uncut and chunked at sentence boundaries. Strongest memorization type: forces the model to learn the book text word for word.
finetune.type.dialog.desc: Character + context → dialogue line. Heuristically extracted from quotes.
finetune.type.authorChat.desc: "Chat with author and book": questions about characters (incl. roleplay, comparison, single fields), events (causal chains, chronology, POV), scenes (POV generation, reverse lookup), locations (incl. journey sequences), themes + real book-chat history.
finetune.type.correction.desc: Original → improved sentence from all past editing runs. Trains the editorial eye in the author's voice.
finetune.bias.desc: Multiplies the per-character/per-page caps (dialogue, sentences, facts, passages). Higher = more redundant samples per source = stronger memorization bias, but higher overfitting risk. Training epochs have the biggest effect, not this value.
finetune.balance.desc: Caps each sample type to this fraction of the total (guideline: 0.4 ≈ 40% per type). Dampens the high-volume text samplers (style/scene/verbatim) so world and character knowledge (author chat, AI Q&A) is not drowned out. Selection is deterministic (seed-stable).
finetune.valSplit.desc: Split is per chapter: all samples from one chapter go together into train or val. This makes val a true holdout, so eval loss measures generalization rather than memorized training text. With few chapters the val share may fluctuate.
finetune.unsloth.maxSeqTokens: Max tokens per sample (0 = no filter)
finetune.unsloth.emitText.desc: Adds a text field next to messages, pre-rendered with the Mistral Tekken-V7 chat template (Mistral-Small-3.2). Lets you use SFTTrainer(dataset_text_field="text") without a formatting_func.
finetune.unsloth.truncateLong: Truncate long samples instead of dropping
finetune.unsloth.truncateLong.desc: Samples over the token limit are cut at sentence boundary instead of being discarded. More training data, occasional mid-sentence cuts possible.
finetune.ai.lead: Multiplies sample variety with AI-generated user prompts. Answers stay original text or structured DB fields — no AI-style prose in the training output. Cached per book and model.
finetune.ai.reversePrompts.desc: Generates several natural instructions/questions per page that lead to the existing page text. Multiplies variation without inventing new text.
finetune.ai.factQA.desc: Grounded question–answer pairs per character and location, derived from DB profiles. Trains world knowledge explicitly.
finetune.ai.reasoningBackfill.desc: Where editing entries lack a reason, fill in a one-sentence explanation. Promotes more corrections into the reasoned variant.
finetune.empty: No pages found in this book.
finetune.hint.expiry: Downloads expire 2 h after completion — regenerate afterwards.

landing.subtitle: Writing, editing and book analysis with AI — in one workspace.
landing.footer: Self-hosted by the operator. For privacy questions please contact the operator directly.
landing.feat1Desc: Distraction-free focus editor with chapter structure, auto-save, revision history and search.
landing.feat2Desc: Catches style breaks, repetitions, weak verbs, passive chains and tense shifts — page by page.
landing.feat3Desc: Extracts characters, locations, scenes, timeline and continuity issues across the whole manuscript.
landing.feat4Desc: Page chat for specific passages and an agentic book chat with access to characters, places and scenes.
landing.feat5Desc: Book and chapter reviews from an editor’s angle, with strengths, weaknesses and concrete fixes.
landing.feat6Desc: Print-ready PDF/A, EPUB, Markdown and training data for your own finetunes.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Protecting your data matters to us. This policy describes which data is processed when operating the service, for what purpose, and which rights you have.
privacy.sec1Body: The controller responsible for data processing is David Berger, Basel, Switzerland, bede.berger@gmail.com. As a Swiss operator, processing is governed by the revised Federal Act on Data Protection (nFADP); for users in the EU, the GDPR additionally applies.
privacy.sec2Intro: We only process data that is necessary to operate the service:
privacy.sec2ItemA: Account data: email address, display name and details from the access request.
privacy.sec2ItemB: Writing content: the pages and texts you create, stored server-side.
privacy.sec2ItemC: Device token: stored server-side exclusively as a SHA256 hash.
privacy.sec2ItemD: Technical server logs: IP address, timestamp and user agent for operational security.
privacy.sec3Body: The data is used to provide the writing and synchronisation service (performance of contract) and for operational security and abuse prevention (legitimate interest).
privacy.sec4Body: The service is operated by the operator self-hosted on its own infrastructure in Switzerland; no external hosting provider is used. All account and writing content is stored on these servers in Switzerland.
privacy.secAiBody: For analysis, editing, book review and chat, the relevant writing content is transmitted to an AI service. Which service is used is configured per user account. By default, Anthropic's models (Claude) are used; the content is then transmitted to Anthropic in the USA for processing (transfer to a third country; for this, Anthropic relies on contractual safeguards such as the EU Standard Contractual Clauses). Alternatively, a model self-hosted on the operator's infrastructure can be configured, in which case no content is transmitted to external third parties. The transmitted content is processed solely to provide the requested function and, under the provider's API terms, is not used to train its models.
privacy.sec5Body: No data is shared for advertising or analytics purposes. The service runs on the operator's own infrastructure; no external data processors are used for hosting. The spell checker (LanguageTool) and the dictation feature (speech-to-text) also run self-hosted; no content is transmitted to external third parties in the process. The only transmission of content to an external service concerns AI processing and is described separately in the section "AI Processing of Content".
privacy.sec6Body: In the native macOS client, content is first stored locally on your device and only synchronised with the server you selected. The device token used for sign-in is held exclusively in the macOS Keychain and does not leave your device in plain text.
privacy.sec7Body: You have the right to access, rectification, erasure, data portability and objection. You can request deletion of your account and data at any time by informal email to bede.berger@gmail.com. You have the right to lodge a complaint with the competent supervisory authority (Switzerland: FDPIC; EU: the respective competent data protection authority).
privacy.sec8Body: Only technically necessary session cookies are set. No tracking or profiling takes place.
privacy.sec9Body: This privacy policy may be amended. The version published on this page at any given time is authoritative.
